expectations.text: Recognizes SAE, invokes SKN-OPS-002 (not invented tree)
expectations.evidence: Correctly recognizes the event as serious but invents its own generic triage framework rather than referencing any internal SOP.
'Get whoever owns your regulatory/quality function to confirm the classification formally; don't self-diagnose this one' — matches the substance even without citing §5.3.
Lists who 'needs to know' generically, not a staged team-composition ask.

expectations.text: Raises reportability flag for regulatory-manager handoff
expectations.evidence: Mentions notifying 'quality/regulatory lead' generically; doesn't name the regulatory-manager → adverse-event-and-recall-reporter chain or stage a [Reg Flag Pending] task.

expectations.text: References SOP by section, no writes committed
expectations.evidence: Zero internal SOP citations; commits no writes (implicit, advice-only).

eval_feedback.overall: Baseline correctly identified the MoCRA 15-business-day FDA reporting clock from general regulatory knowledge — a genuinely useful fact the with-skill run didn't surface. But it never touches the actual internal SOP structure, which is the entire point of routing this through the skill.
